message:
fix(storage): ref_category no longer mis-classifies refs/stashy/* as SafeToPrune

The SAFE_PREFIXES list in `ref_category` had `"refs/stash"` without a
trailing slash, alongside `"refs/temp/"` and `"refs/original/"` (both
with slashes). Combined with the `starts_with` check, that meant ANY
ref whose name began with the literal string `refs/stash` — including
nonstandard names like `refs/stashy/foo` or `refs/stash-backup` —
would be classified as SafeToPrune and auto-pruned by
`am doctor fix-orphan-refs --apply`.

Real-world impact is small since git itself never creates refs under
`refs/stash/` (the stash uses a single tip ref with reflog, not a
namespace), but a user who ad-hoc named a branch `refs/stashy/wip`
and had it get orphaned would see it silently disappear.

Fixed by moving `refs/stash` out of the prefix loop entirely — the
exact-match short-circuit above the loop already handles the one
legitimate case. Added a regression test covering refs/stashy/foo
and refs/stash-backup.
